name: Drupal Ecosystem
description: Drupal is more than a CMS — it is a framework, a global community of initiatives and local organizations, and an ecosystem of companies that build products and services around it.

children.name: Build with Drupal
children.description: Drupal Core, contributed modules, and the tools developers use to build and run Drupal sites.

children.name: Drupal Core
children.description: The core software: PHP framework, content modeling, rendering, configuration, and the foundational modules every Drupal site uses.

children.name: Action
children.description: Trigger configurable actions on events (publish, email, etc.).

children.name: Block
children.description: Place reusable content blocks into theme regions.

children.name: Book
children.description: Build hierarchical, multi-page documentation structures.

children.name: Breakpoint
children.description: Manage responsive-design breakpoints used by themes and media.

children.name: CKEditor 5
children.description: Rich-text WYSIWYG editor integration (current default).

children.name: Configuration
children.description: Manage site configuration via importable/exportable YAML.

children.name: Contact
children.description: Site-wide and per-user contact forms.

children.name: Contextual Links
children.description: Inline edit links that appear on hover for editors.

children.name: Datetime
children.description: Date and time field type plus widgets and formatters.

children.name: Database Logging
children.description: Record events to the database (the watchdog log).

children.name: Editor
children.description: Framework that text-format WYSIWYG editors plug into.

children.name: Field
children.description: The Field API: attach typed data to any entity.

children.name: File
children.description: Managed file uploads and the file field type.

children.name: Help
children.description: Module-provided help pages and tooltips.

children.name: History
children.description: Track which nodes a user has read; powers "new" markers.

children.name: Image
children.description: Image field type, image styles, derivative generation.

children.name: JSON:API
children.description: Spec-compliant read/write JSON:API endpoints for every entity.

children.name: Layout Builder
children.description: Visual page-layout composition with blocks and sections.

children.name: Link
children.description: Link field type with internal/external URL handling.

children.name: Locale
children.description: Translation management for interface strings and configuration.

children.name: Media
children.description: Reusable media entities (images, video, audio, remote embeds).

children.name: Migrate
children.description: ETL framework for migrating data into Drupal from any source.

children.name: Navigation
children.description: The modern admin navigation bar (Drupal 11+).

children.name: Node
children.description: Content entity API: pages, articles, custom content types.

children.name: Page Cache
children.description: Cache full pages for anonymous users.

children.name: REST
children.description: Configurable REST endpoints for entities and views.

children.name: Search
children.description: Basic search index + search pages.

children.name: Text
children.description: Plain and formatted text field types.

children.name: User
children.description: Accounts, roles, permissions — the user entity.

children.name: Views
children.description: Build listings, grids, blocks, and feeds with a query UI.

children.name: Contributed modules
children.description: Tens of thousands of community-maintained modules and themes that extend Drupal — from Views and Paragraphs to commerce, search, and AI integrations. Grouped here by topic with a handful of well-known examples per group.

children.name: Admin & editor UX
children.description: Modules that improve the editorial and administrative experience.

children.name: Admin Toolbar
children.description: Replaces the default admin menu with a faster, dropdown-driven version. Near-universal install.

children.name: Diff
children.description: Visual diff between two revisions of any entity.

children.name: Security & spam
children.description: Modules that harden Drupal sites against abuse.

children.name: CAPTCHA
children.description: Pluggable challenge framework with reCAPTCHA, image, math integrations.

children.name: Honeypot
children.description: Lightweight spam-bot defense via hidden form fields and time traps.

children.name: Developer tools
children.description: Modules every Drupal developer reaches for.

children.name: Chaos Tools (ctools)
children.description: Long-running utility library — plugin systems, wizards, object caching — depended on by countless other modules.

children.name: Devel
children.description: Developer utility belt: variable dumps, generators, query log, Drush integration.

children.name: Media & embeds
children.description: Modules that extend Drupal's media handling.

children.name: Embed
children.description: Framework for embedding entities and external media into CKEditor.

children.name: Analytics & marketing
children.description: Modules that connect Drupal to analytics and marketing tooling.

children.name: Google Tag
children.description: Integrate Google Tag Manager containers with conditional injection rules.

children.name: Commerce
children.description: Placeholder — the Drupal Commerce ecosystem (Commerce module + payment/shipping/tax extensions) to be expanded later.

children.name: Themes & UI
children.description: Placeholder — front-end themes (Olivero, Claro, Radix, Bootstrap-based, Tailwind-based) to be expanded later.

children.name: Tools
children.description: Command-line, local-development, packaging, and scaffolding tools the Drupal community builds and shares.

children.name: Drush
children.description: The Drupal Shell — the standard command-line tool for administering Drupal sites: cache clears, config sync, user management, scripting.

children.name: DDEV
children.description: Docker-based local development environment recommended by Drupal's official Local Development Guide; the dominant choice for new Drupal projects.

children.name: Composer
children.description: PHP dependency manager — the supported way to install, update, and patch Drupal Core and contributed projects.

children.name: Lando
children.description: Another Docker-based local-dev environment popular with Drupal teams, particularly those coming from Pantheon-style workflows.

children.name: Drupal Console
children.description: Symfony-Console-based scaffolding CLI. Widely used during Drupal 8/9 era; largely superseded by Drush + Composer + Recipes today.

children.name: Recipes
children.description: Drupal 10.3+ mechanism for installable, repeatable site configurations — modules, config, and content together. Powers Drupal CMS.

children.name: api.drupal.org
children.description: Generated API documentation site for Drupal Core and major contributed modules — the developer reference.

children.name: Drupal CMS
children.description: Drupal's official 2025 distribution targeting marketers, designers, and site builders with smart defaults, recipes, and a guided setup. The fastest way for a non-developer to stand up a Drupal site.

children.name: Drupal CRM
children.description: Upcoming Drupal Association product (announced after Drupal CMS) — a marketing CRM built on Drupal aimed at organizations who want their customer data and editorial system in one place. Placeholder until the official launch page lands.

children.name: Drupal Commerce
children.description: Mature e-commerce framework for Drupal, maintained by Centarro. Powers everything from boutique stores to enterprise commerce on top of Drupal's content modeling.

children.name: Community
children.description: The people, initiatives, and organizations behind Drupal — globally and regionally.

children.name: Initiatives & Working Groups
children.description: Project-wide efforts and working groups coordinated by community members and the Drupal Association.

children.name: Composer Support in Core
children.description: Initiative making Composer the supported way to install and update Drupal Core.

children.name: Drupal AI
children.description: Contributed modules and an associated initiative bringing AI capabilities into Drupal site building and editorial workflows.

children.name: IXP Fellowships
children.description: Innovation eXchange Program — provides incentives to companies that train inexperienced developers and contribute back.

children.name: Community Working Group
children.description: Volunteer group that supports the health of the Drupal community and upholds the Drupal Code of Conduct.

children.name: Mentoring
children.description: Working group supporting mentors and organising mentoring activities — especially at DrupalCons and DrupalCamps.

children.name: Diversity & Inclusion
children.description: Drupal Diversity & Inclusion — continuing conversations and concrete programs on diversity and inclusion within the project.

children.name: Promote Drupal
children.description: Marketing materials and resources for agencies growing Drupal adoption.

children.name: Contribution Events
children.description: Promotes contribution sprints and code-together events globally.

children.name: Event Organizers' Group
children.description: Supports people organising Drupal-related events worldwide — camps, meetups, summits.

children.name: Local Associations
children.description: Regional entities organising local efforts, promoting business, and coordinating with the global Drupal Association.

children.name: Nonprofit Drupal
children.description: Community group for people working in or with nonprofits using Drupal.

children.name: NEDA — Network of European Drupal Agencies
children.description: European agencies collaborating to advance Drupal development and shared infrastructure.

children.name: Global Training Days
children.description: Worldwide free-training events recommended for newcomers to Drupal.

children.name: Organizations
children.description: Regional and national associations that organize the community on the ground.

children.name: United States
children.description: US-based Drupal community organizations and their events.

children.name: Drupal Association
children.description: The non-profit that supports the Drupal project worldwide, runs drupal.org, and organizes the flagship DrupalCon conferences.

children.name: DrupalCon North America
children.description: Annual flagship North American conference organized by the Drupal Association since 2005.
Portland, OR — ~100 attendees.
Vancouver, BC — ~150 attendees.
Sunnyvale, CA — ~300 attendees.
Boston, MA — 850 attendees.
Washington, D.C. — 1,400 attendees.
San Francisco, CA — 3,000 attendees.
Chicago, IL — 2,881 attendees.
Denver, CO — 3,127 attendees.
Portland, OR — 3,300 attendees.
Austin, TX — 3,357 attendees.
Los Angeles, CA — 3,186 attendees.
New Orleans, LA — 3,102 attendees.
Baltimore, MD — 3,271 attendees.
Nashville, TN — 2,899 attendees.
Seattle, WA — 3,014 attendees.
Online (Global) — 2,395 attendees.
Online (North America) — 2,330 attendees.
Portland, OR — 1,263 attendees.
Pittsburgh, PA — 1,406 attendees.
Portland, OR — 1,400 attendees.
Atlanta, GA — 1,288 attendees.
Chicago, IL — upcoming.

children.name: Florida
children.description: Regional Drupal community in Florida; organizes Florida DrupalCamp annually.

children.name: Florida DrupalCamp
children.description: Annual regional DrupalCamp in Florida.

children.name: Texas
children.description: Regional Drupal community in Texas; rotates TexasCamp between Austin, Dallas, and San Antonio.

children.name: TexasCamp
children.description: Annual statewide Texas Drupal camp (Dallas 2016, Austin 2017, San Antonio 2019).

children.name: Boston / New England
children.description: Boston Drupal Users Group and the broader New England Drupal community.

children.name: New York City
children.description: Drupal NYC — long-running NYC user group; has hosted NYC Camp.

children.name: Chicago
children.description: Drupal Chicago user group; hosted DrupalCon Chicago 2011 and again in 2026.

children.name: Bay Area
children.description: San Francisco Bay Area Drupal community; historic home of BADCamp.

children.name: Pacific Northwest
children.description: Portland + Seattle communities; Portland has hosted DrupalCon NA four times (2005, 2013, 2022, 2024).

children.name: Denver / Colorado
children.description: Colorado Drupal community; hosted DrupalCon Denver 2012.

children.name: Capital (DC area)
children.description: Washington DC / Maryland / Virginia community; hosts the annual Capital Camp.

children.name: Twin Cities
children.description: Minneapolis–Saint Paul Drupal community.

children.name: Atlanta
children.description: Atlanta Drupal community; hosted DrupalCon NA 2025.

children.name: Europe
children.description: European Drupal community organizations and their events.

children.name: Germany
children.description: Drupal Deutschland e.V. plus camps in Munich, Berlin, and Frankfurt. Hosted DrupalCon Munich 2012.

children.name: Netherlands
children.description: Dutch Drupal community; organises DrupalJam. Amsterdam hosted DrupalCon Europe in 2005, 2014, 2019; Rotterdam in 2026.

children.name: United Kingdom
children.description: DrupalCamp London and the wider UK community. London hosted DrupalCon Europe 2011.

children.name: France
children.description: French Drupal Association; hosted DrupalCon Europe in Paris 2009 and Lille 2023.

children.name: Spain
children.description: DrupalCamp Spain runs annually; Barcelona hosted DrupalCon Europe in 2007, 2015, and 2024.

children.name: Italy
children.description: Italian Drupal community; organises DrupalDay Italia.

children.name: Belgium
children.description: DUG BE vzw (Drupal User Group Belgium). Antwerp hosted the very first DrupalCon in 2005; Brussels in 2006.

children.name: Austria
children.description: Austrian Drupal community; Vienna hosted DrupalCon Europe in 2017 and 2025.

children.name: Czech Republic
children.description: Czech Drupal community; Prague hosted DrupalCon Europe in 2013 and 2022.

children.name: Ireland
children.description: Irish Drupal community; Dublin hosted DrupalCon Europe 2016.

children.name: Hungary
children.description: Hungarian Drupal community; Szeged hosted DrupalCon Europe 2008.

children.name: Denmark
children.description: Danish Drupal community; Copenhagen hosted DrupalCon Europe 2010.

children.name: Poland
children.description: Polish Drupal community; organises DrupalCamp Poland (2026 confirmed).

children.name: Finland
children.description: Finnish Drupal community; organises DrupalCamp Finland (2026 confirmed).

children.name: Switzerland
children.description: Swiss Drupal community.

children.name: European Drupal Association
children.description: Coordinates the European DrupalCon and supports regional camps across Europe.

children.name: DrupalCon Europe
children.description: Annual flagship European conference. The European DrupalCon series has run continuously since 2005 (no Association-organized edition in 2018).

children.name: 2005·Antwerp
children.description: Antwerp, Belgium — the very first DrupalCon (~50 attendees).

children.name: 2005·Amsterdam
children.description: Amsterdam, Netherlands — ~100 attendees.
Brussels, Belgium — 150 attendees.
Barcelona, Spain — 450 attendees.
Szeged, Hungary — 500 attendees.
Paris, France — 850 attendees.
Copenhagen, Denmark — 1,200 attendees.
London, UK — 1,751 attendees.
Munich, Germany — 1,700 attendees.
Prague, Czech Republic — 1,830 attendees.
Amsterdam, Netherlands — 2,300 attendees.

Dublin, Ireland — 1,787 attendees.
Vienna, Austria — 1,670 attendees.
Amsterdam, Netherlands — 1,500 attendees.
Online — 780 attendees.
Online — 934 attendees.

Lille, France — 1,289 attendees.

children.name: Latin America
children.description: Drupal community organizations and camps across Latin America.

children.name: Costa Rica
children.description: Active Costa Rican Drupal community; organizes the region's largest free Drupal-and-open-source camp each February.

children.name: DrupalCamp Costa Rica
children.description: Three-day annual camp held at the University of Costa Rica (Liberia, Guanacaste). Workshops, contribution sprints, talks on Drupal, AI, and the open web — free to attend.

children.name: Brasil
children.description: Brazilian Drupal community reigniting around DrupalCamp Brasil; bridges Drupal with the wider Brazilian PHP ecosystem.

children.name: DrupalCamp Brasil
children.description: Camp held in southern Brazil (Santa Catarina region) to reconnect Brazilian contributors, agencies, and newcomers.

children.name: México
children.description: Mexican Drupal community centered in Guadalajara; organizes DrupalCamp México.

children.name: DrupalCamp México
children.description: Mexican community camp organized out of Guadalajara.

children.name: Asia
children.description: Asian Drupal communities and regional DrupalCons.

children.name: India
children.description: Active Indian Drupal community; hosted DrupalCon Asia in Mumbai.

children.name: DrupalCon Asia 2016
children.description: Mumbai, India — 1,025 attendees. The first DrupalCon held in Asia.

children.name: Singapore / SEA
children.description: Singapore and Southeast Asia Drupal community.

children.name: DrupalCon Singapore 2024
children.description: Singapore — 253 attendees.

children.name: Japan
children.description: Japanese Drupal community.

children.name: DrupalCon Nara 2025
children.description: Nara, Japan — 214 attendees.

children.name: Oceania
children.description: Drupal community organizations and events across Australia and New Zealand.

children.name: Australia
children.description: Australian Drupal community; hosts DrupalSouth and previously DrupalCon Sydney.

children.name: DrupalCon Sydney 2013
children.description: Sydney, Australia — 450 attendees.

children.name: DrupalSouth (AU)
children.description: Twice-yearly regional conference rotating across Australia and New Zealand cities. Placeholder — to be expanded with per-edition history.

children.name: New Zealand
children.description: New Zealand Drupal community; co-hosts the DrupalSouth conference series with Australia.

children.name: DrupalSouth NZ (TBD)
children.description: Placeholder — specific NZ DrupalSouth editions to be added with verified data.

children.name: Africa
children.description: Drupal communities and camps across Africa.

children.name: South Africa
children.description: Placeholder — to be verified and filled with the active South African Drupal community contacts and events.

children.name: DrupalCamp SA (TBD)
children.description: Placeholder — to be replaced with verified DrupalCamp South Africa editions.

children.name: Other African camps (TBD)
children.description: Placeholder — Drupal camps and meetups elsewhere on the continent (Nigeria, Kenya, Egypt, Morocco, etc.) to be added as verified.

children.name: Partners and Services
children.description: Companies that build products, sell services, sponsor events, and employ Drupal contributors.

children.name: United States
children.description: US-based Drupal companies.

children.name: Acquia
children.description: The largest commercial Drupal-focused vendor; provides hosting, digital experience products, and is a long-time Drupal Association sponsor.

children.name: Pantheon
children.description: Web ops platform for Drupal (and WordPress); hosting, workflow tooling, and CDN.

children.name: Palantir.net
children.description: Chicago-based Drupal agency serving higher-ed, healthcare, and large publishers.

children.name: Four Kitchens
children.description: Distributed Drupal agency working on large-scale publishing and government sites.

children.name: Tag1 Consulting
children.description: Engineering-heavy consultancy specializing in Drupal performance, scaling, and migrations.

children.name: Kanopi Studios
children.description: Design and Drupal/WordPress agency focused on mission-driven organizations.

children.name: Latin America
children.description: Latin American Drupal companies.

children.name: Seed
children.description: Colombia-based Drupal team.

children.name: ParallelDev
children.description: Costa Rica-based Drupal team.

children.name: Bluefly
children.description: A platform sustaining independent developers and small companies who build community, tools, and products across the Drupal ecosystem.

children.name: Independent Developers
children.description: Individual contributors and freelancers whose work Bluefly supports.

children.name: Examples (TBD)
children.description: Independent developers to be named here with their consent.

children.name: Small Companies
children.description: Small Drupal-focused companies whose work Bluefly supports.

children.name: Examples (TBD)
children.description: Small companies to be named here with their consent.
